senses.definition: to have in your hands or arms

examples.x: They held candles during the power outage.
She held her friend's hand for support during the scary movie.
He gently held the fragile vase, careful not to drop it.
As the team captain, she proudly held the championship trophy.

senses.definition: to be able to say who or what someone or something is

examples.x: The detective easily identifies the suspect from the security footage.
The officer asked me to identify my belongings at the lost-and-found desk.
The teacher identified the student's handwriting on the exam.
I couldn’t identify the song at first, but then I recognized it.
She couldn’t identify the person at the door until they spoke.

senses.definition: to be part of an event, situation, or activity

examples.x: It’s crazy to make these changes without involving the students.
Parents should involve themselves in their child's education.
The goal is to involve workers in the decision-making process.
Try to involve as many children as possible in the game.
We want to involve as many people as possible in the celebrations.
We want to involve the workforce at all stages of the decision-making process.

senses.definition: to say something funny or behave in a way that makes people laugh

examples.x: He joked about the funny incident that happened earlier.
We often joke about our shared experiences.
The students joked with their teacher during class.
He joked about the long meeting to lighten the mood.
The teacher joked that the homework would be graded by the class pet.

senses.definition: to hit a door, surface, etc. in a way to attract attention, especially expecting it to be opened

examples.x: She had to knock on the door to announce her arrival.
The neighbor decided to knock on the door to return the borrowed sugar.
In the middle of the night, a mysterious sound made her nervous, so she cautiously knocked on her roommate's door.
Before entering the office, it's polite to knock on the door to ensure you're not interrupting a meeting.
The friend didn't have a phone, so she had to knock on the window to get the homeowner's attention.

senses.definition: to guide or show the direction for others to follow

examples.x: The tour guide led us through the museum.
Dad led the family on a hike in the forest.
The teacher led the students to the classroom.
The tour guide will lead us through the historic landmarks of the city.
Please follow me, and I'll lead you to the conference room.

senses.definition: to move a thing from a lower position or level to a higher one

examples.x: Every morning, she lifts weights at the gym for strength training.
The construction worker lifts heavy bricks onto the scaffold.
The forklift operator is lifting pallets of goods in the warehouse.
The crane is lifting steel beams for the construction project.
The team has lifted the trophy after winning the championship.

senses.definition: to set something on fire

examples.x: She lit the candles on the birthday cake with a match.
They lit the campfire with a lighter to cook dinner outdoors.
He lights the fireplace to warm up the room on a chilly evening.
She lights the incense sticks to create a relaxing atmosphere in the room.

senses.definition: to establish a physical connection or attachment between two or more things

examples.x: The bridge links the island to the mainland, providing a route for vehicles and pedestrians.
The rope ladder links the lower deck of the ship to the upper deck.
The train tracks link cities and towns, facilitating transportation between them.
The zipper links the two sides of the jacket, keeping it closed in cold weather.
The pipeline links the oil field to the refinery, transporting crude oil for processing.

senses.definition: to secure something with a lock or seal

examples.x: Before leaving, he had to lock the front door to ensure the security of the house.
The cyclist always makes sure to lock the bike to the rack before leaving.
The manager instructed the staff to lock the office doors after working hours.
She locks the front door every night before going to bed.
They locked the windows during the storm last night.

senses.definition: to leave a sign, line, etc. on something

examples.x: Please use a pencil to mark the location where the measurements should be taken.
She carefully marked the important passages in the textbook with colored tabs.
Before cutting the fabric, the tailor used chalk to mark the pattern.
The athlete used a marker to mark the starting line of the race.

senses.definition: to be important or have a great effect on someone or something

examples.x: It doesn't matter what time you arrive; the event will still start at 7 PM.
The details of the contract negotiation will matter during the final decision-making process.
When choosing a career, personal fulfillment and passion often matter more than monetary gain.

senses.definition: to say something about someone or something, without giving much detail

examples.x: During the meeting, please mention any concerns or suggestions you may have.
He didn't mention the party until the last minute, catching us by surprise.
Can you mention where you found that interesting article?
In her speech, the speaker will mention the importance of community engagement.
If you have any dietary restrictions, please mention them when making the reservation.

senses.definition: (often used in negative or question form) to be upset, offended, or bothered by something

examples.x: I don't mind if you borrow my book, just please return it when you're done.
He minded the noise from the construction work next door, as it disturbed his concentration.
He doesn't mind if people disagree with him; he welcomes different perspectives.
She didn't mind waiting a few minutes for her friend to arrive.
Does she mind if we use her laptop to finish the project?

senses.definition: to give numbers to different parts of a list or series of objects or people

examples.x: The teacher numbered the pages of the exam to ensure they were in the correct order.
Please number your answers on the worksheet from 1 to 10.
The librarian numbered the books on the shelf for easier organization.
Each participant was numbered for the marathon race to track their progress.
The parcels were numbered for efficient delivery routing.

senses.definition: to make the necessary arrangements for an event or activity to take place

examples.x: I organize the annual charity event for our community.
You organize the logistics for the conference every year.
He organizes the schedule for the team meetings.
We are organizing a surprise party for her birthday.
The committee is organizing the agenda for the upcoming summit.

senses.definition: to have something as for ourselves

examples.x: She currently owns a small business in the downtown area.
The family proudly owns a historic farmhouse.
Last year, he successfully owned a vintage car that he had restored.
The company owned several patents for their innovative technology.

senses.definition: to put clothes and other things needed for travel into a bag, suitcase, etc.

examples.x: Before the trip, she had to pack her suitcase with essentials.
Yesterday, she packed her suitcase for the business trip.
They are currently packing their suitcases for the weekend trip.
Before leaving for the trip, he packed his backpack with snacks, water, and a map.
They packed their carry-on bags with essential items for the long flight ahead.

senses.definition: to use a camera to take a picture of something

examples.x: He photographs street scenes in urban areas.
She photographs landscapes in her free time.
The traveler photographs landmarks while exploring new cities.
The journalist photographs events for the newspaper.
He photographed wildlife during his travels.

senses.definition: to say that something is going to happen before it actually takes place

examples.x: The meteorologist predicted rain for the weekend based on weather patterns.
Economists predict an increase in market volatility due to current economic trends.
He predicted the success of the business venture based on market analysis.
The fortune teller claimed to predict future events through mystical means.
She accurately predicted the outcome of the election based on polling data.

senses.definition: to give something to someone as a gift, often in a formal manner

examples.x: He decided to present his sister with a beautiful necklace for her birthday.
As a token of appreciation, the company president presented each employee with a gift at the annual awards ceremony.
As a sign of gratitude, the organization decided to present the volunteers with certificates of appreciation.
The academy will present the Nobel Prize in Literature to the renowned author for his outstanding contributions to literature.
The school board will present certificates of achievement to the top-performing students at the graduation ceremony.

senses.definition: to not let someone do something

examples.x: The security guard prevented the unauthorized person from entering the building.
She tried to prevent her younger brother from eating too much candy before dinner.
The referee intervened to prevent the players from escalating the argument into a physical altercation.
She took precautions to prevent her child from getting lost in the crowded mall.
Right now, the police are taking action to prevent the protest from escalating.

senses.definition: to make words, pictures, or anything else on something such as a piece of paper using ink and a special device called printer

examples.x: I printed the document on a piece of paper so I could review it later.
He needed to print the boarding pass before heading to the airport.
He prints the boarding passes before heading to the airport.
The company prints brochures to distribute at trade shows.
He printed a copy of the recipe for each of his friends.

senses.definition: to tell someone that one will do something or that a particular event will happen

examples.x: He promised to help her with the project last week.
She promises to attend the meeting every Monday.
While on the campaign trail, the candidate was promising various improvements to the community.
She promised her daughter a trip to the amusement park if she did well in her exams.
The company promised its shareholders increased dividends following a successful quarter.
They promised their parents that they would call them every week while studying abroad.
He promised his best friend that he would be his best man at the wedding.

senses.definition: to compete against someone to see who is the fastest

examples.x: The children race each other to the tree.
They are racing to see who reaches the hilltop first.
Tomorrow, I will race in the city marathon.

senses.definition: to close something

examples.x: Before going to bed, she had to shut all the windows to keep the cold air out.
The chef had to shut the oven door tightly to bake the perfect cake.
The caretaker shuts the gates to the park every evening.
She is shutting the store for the night.
He shut the book when he finished reading.

senses.definition: to show the correct way or place to someone

examples.x: The map will guide you to the destination.
The experienced hiker guided the group through the mountain trails.
GPS devices guide drivers to their intended destinations.
A lighthouse serves to guide ships safely into the harbor.

senses.definition: to end the life of someone or something

examples.x: The hunter aimed carefully to kill the deer for food.
Pesticides are used in agriculture to kill harmful insects.
The murderer used a weapon to kill his victim.
The chef had to kill the lobster before cooking it.
The assassin was hired to kill a political figure.

senses.definition: to adjust something to be in a suitable or desired condition for a specific purpose or use

examples.x: She set the computer to mute.
Set the oven timer for 30 minutes.
The barista set the coffee machine for a double espresso.
He set the thermostat to 72 degrees to keep the room warm.
Before leaving, don't forget to set your watch to the correct time zone.
She set the camera to manual mode for more creative control.
He set the radio volume to low.

senses.definition: to move a car, bus, etc. into an empty place and leave it there for a short time

examples.x: After circling the block for several minutes, she finally found a spot to park her car.
The bus driver skillfully parked the bus at the designated stop.
The commuters hurriedly parked their bicycles in the designated area before catching the train.
The valet attendant quickly parked the guests' cars in an organized row.
As the family reached the amusement park, they began looking for a suitable place to park their minivan.
